commits: 5799f4d: test(0.8.2/E2): RED — rerank_passages helper reorders CE-disagrees pool + depth0/empty identity
94732bb: feat(0.8.2/E2): GREEN — standalone rerank SDK API over an arbitrary passage list (engine helper + pyo3 fn + governed-surface registration + DOC-INDEX)

api.engine_helper: pub fn rerank_passages(query: &str, passages: Vec<(u64, String, f64)>, rerank_depth: usize) -> Vec<(u64, f64)>  (fathomdb-engine/src/lib.rs, immediately after rerank_fused; pure, calls rerank_fused; adapts each passage into SearchHit{kind="passage", branch=Vector, source_id=None} — only body+score feed the blend — then projects the reranked SearchHits back to (id, score))
api.pyo3_module_fn: fathomdb.rerank(query: str, passages: list[dict], rerank_depth: int) -> list[dict]
api.input_shape: passages = list of {"id": int (>=0), "body": str, "score": float}  (score = the caller's fused/RRF score)
api.output_shape: list of {"id": int, "score": float} in the RERANKED order; output score is the CE-blended score (alpha=0.3 x sigmoid(CE) + 0.7 x RRF-norm over the top-depth pool)
api.shape_decision: Module-level free function (not Engine.rerank) chosen because the rerank is engine-FREE: it operates on a caller-supplied pool with no DB/engine handle, exactly what Slice 5's in-harness fused(bm25+dense) pool needs. Output carries (id, score) only — sufficient for the comparator to reorder its own pool (body is keyed by id in the harness); kept minimal per the thin-wrapper mandate and matches the pure helper's Vec<(id,score)> return verbatim.
api.registration: Native fn registered in the _fathomdb #[pymodule] (m.add_function(wrap_pyfunction!(rerank, &m))). Re-exported as fathomdb.rerank in src/python/fathomdb/__init__.py (import + __all__). Type stub added to src/python/fathomdb/_fathomdb.pyi. Governed-surface allowlist member 'rerank' added to the single shared src/conformance/governed-surface-allowlist.json.

tdd_evidence.red_evidence: At RED (5799f4d) the new feature-gated test target tests/pr_e2_rerank_passages.rs references fathomdb_engine::rerank_passages which does not yet exist => `cargo test -p fathomdb-engine --features default-reranker --test pr_e2_rerank_passages` FAILS to compile: error[E0432] unresolved import `fathomdb_engine::rerank_passages` (no `rerank_passages` in the root). GREEN at 94732bb: same command => 3 passed (reorders id=2 to top via real TinyBERT-L-2 CE; depth0 identity; empty identity).

contracts_confirmed.depth0_identity: rerank_passages(_, input, 0) returns (id, score) in input order with input scores byte-identical — inherited from rerank_fused's depth==0 short-circuit (BEFORE any #[cfg(default-reranker)] block). Verified feature-on by rerank_passages_depth_0_is_identity (green).
contracts_confirmed.empty_identity: rerank_passages(_, [], depth) returns [] immediately — inherited from rerank_fused/ce_rerank empty guard; no model load. Verified by rerank_passages_empty_is_identity (green).
contracts_confirmed.no_network: depth==0 and empty short-circuit before the CE singleton is touched; with the feature OFF the entire CE path compiles away (rerank_fused is always identity). The only network is the existing E1 gated ~17MB cache-miss fetch on first rerank_depth>0 with weights absent — unchanged by E2.
contracts_confirmed.feature_off_candle_free: fathomdb-py default features = [] and the rerank wrapper references nothing candle-specific (only rerank_passages/rerank_fused which exist in both feature states). `cargo tree -p fathomdb-py --features pyo3/extension-module -e features | grep -ci candle` => 0. Feature-off `cargo clippy -p fathomdb-engine -p fathomdb-py -- -D warnings` clean.
contracts_confirmed.never_panics: pyo3 fn returns PyResult; malformed passages (non-dict item, missing/typed-wrong id/body/score) raise the typed WriteValidationError; body rides the FFI string gate (validate_ffi_string_py). The pure helper call is wrapped in catch_unwind(AssertUnwindSafe(..)) inside py.allow_threads, mapping any escaping panic to PanicException (mirrors call_engine).

verification.cargo_test_feature_on_e2: cargo test -p fathomdb-engine --features default-reranker --test pr_e2_rerank_passages => 3 passed (CE reorder id=2->top; depth0 identity; empty identity)
verification.cargo_test_feature_off_reranker: cargo test -p fathomdb-engine --test pr_g10_reranker => 5 passed (soft-fallback/identity/determinism/recall-gate-schema) — E2 helper does not regress the feature-off path
verification.clippy: cargo clippy -p fathomdb-engine -p fathomdb-py --features default-reranker -- -D warnings clean; feature-off clippy clean
verification.fmt: cargo fmt --check clean (pre-commit enforced on both RED and GREEN commits)
verification.candle_free_minimal: cargo tree -p fathomdb-py --features pyo3/extension-module -e features => 0 candle nodes
verification.post_merge: merged --no-ff onto local main at 6884acb (clean; main had not advanced past baseline f151f37). NO push/fetch/PR.

preexisting_failure_noted.symptom: asserts open at SCHEMA_VERSION 14 but fathomdb-schema SCHEMA_VERSION is 15 (left:15 right:14)
preexisting_failure_noted.verdict: PRE-EXISTING at baseline f151f37 and UNRELATED to E2 — E2's diff touches neither the schema crate nor that test; the failure reproduces with E2's GREEN changes stashed. A prior slice bumped SCHEMA_VERSION 14->15 without updating this dependent assertion. Out of scope for E2 ('no other engine behavior'); flagged for the orchestrator to triage/repair separately.
preexisting_failure_noted.evidence: git diff f151f372 --name-only excludes src/rust/crates/fathomdb-schema/ and the test file; with E2 changes stashed (working tree == RED commit, schema unchanged from baseline) the test still FAILS with the same 15-vs-14 assertion.

scope_notes: Touched ONLY: the engine helper rerank_passages (additive pub fn), the feature-gated test target + Cargo.toml gate, the pyo3 `rerank` fn + module registration, the Python __init__/.pyi re-export, the single shared governed-surface allowlist (added 'rerank'), and the DOC-INDEX E1 row (amended to note E2's standalone API). No Slice-5 harness, no other engine behavior, NO TypeScript binding. TS parity NOT ballooned: 'rerank' is added only to the shared allowlist JSON — both Py and TS surface suites do SUBSET checks (live ⊆ allowlist), so the extra allowlist member is safe for both bindings without a TS implementation (Python is what Slice 5 needs); no TS source changed, so no escalation required.
agent_verify_result: pass — RED@5799f4d (helper-missing compile failure), GREEN@94732bb (3 tests green incl. real-CE reorder; depth0/empty identity; never-panics; feature-off candle-free; clippy -D warnings clean both states; fmt clean). Merged --no-ff onto local main at 6884acb. NO push/fetch/PR. Could NOT exercise the Python .so (no maturin in worktree, per mandate) — orchestrator rebuilds + Python-verifies.
next_step_for_orchestrator: rerank API added; orchestrator rebuilds extension + Python-verifies, then Slice 5 fix-1 uses fathomdb.rerank over the fused pool. Specifically: rebuild fathomdb-py with `--features pyo3/extension-module,default-embedder,fathomdb-engine/default-reranker` (maturin develop/build) so the CE path is LIVE, then sanity-check fathomdb.rerank(query, [{id,body,score}...], rerank_depth>0) reorders on real data and depth=0/empty return input order byte-identical (no network); run codex §9 review of the E2 change. SEPARATELY: triage the pre-existing pr_g1_tokenizer_crash_recovery SCHEMA_VERSION 14-vs-15 failure (not introduced by E2).
